title: Post-Edge Pattern chapter-building phase
summary: Twenty-sixth structured packet for Eco Explorer. With `Edge Pattern Line` in flight, the next high-leverage phase is to turn the third route, nursery, front-half exploration, and station board into one stronger chapter of play. The work should review the route, deepen it with transition-driven content fuel, add one short front-half sheltered traversal proof, and then tighten the field-station board for three-route readability and stopping-point cues.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the transition-content matrix for `Edge Pattern Line`, including discoveries, note hooks, comparison anchors, and nursery-aware clue moments.
execution_order.why_now: The third route should be deepened through authored content rather than another new system.

execution_order.goal: Review `Edge Pattern Line` as the first post-nursery route and confirm it reads as a transition chapter.
execution_order.why_now: The route shell needs one clean critique gate before follow-on content grows around it.

execution_order.goal: Deepen `Edge Pattern Line` with transition-driven content fuel.
execution_order.why_now: The route should teach pattern change through discoveries, notes, and comparisons rather than only through board structure.

execution_order.goal: Prepare one front-half sheltered traversal proof for the beach or coastal branch.
execution_order.why_now: The front half of the world still needs its own readable movement identity.

execution_order.goal: Review the transition-content fuel for science quality and route clarity.
execution_order.why_now: This is the point where the route can either deepen the game or just inflate counts.

execution_order.goal: Add one short front-half sheltered traversal proof.
execution_order.why_now: A small optional lower route or shelter lane is the most efficient way to broaden exploration feel without a cave-system rewrite.

execution_order.goal: Prepare three-route field-station board scaling and stopping-point cues.
execution_order.why_now: Once three routes are live, the station needs better episode closure without becoming a dashboard.

execution_order.goal: Review the front-half traversal proof for readability, difficulty, and habitat fit.
execution_order.why_now: The extra traversal identity is valuable only if it stays gentle and readable.

execution_order.goal: Refine the field station for three-route readability and stopping points.
execution_order.why_now: The station now needs one compact chapter-shaping pass more than it needs another new progression system.

execution_order.goal: Review the three-route station board for compactness, warmth, and chapter feel.
execution_order.why_now: The phase is successful only if the station remains a calm notebook base instead of drifting into management UI.

guardrails: Do not add a fourth route in this phase.
Do not broaden the nursery into freeform base-building or a larger resource economy.
Keep front-half traversal curiosity-forward and avoid hard platforming spikes.
Do not generalize into a cave or overhang engine in this phase.
Keep the field station compact, readable at 256x160, and episode-shaped rather than dashboard-like.
Use authored discoveries, notes, prompts, and comparisons to teach transition patterns instead of bolting on a new progression surface.
acceptance_focus: The third route feels like a real transition chapter instead of only another board entry.
The front half of the world gains one readable sheltered traversal identity without a runtime rewrite.
Three-route station play remains calm, compact, and satisfying at 256x160.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-post-edge-pattern-phase.md, and this packet. Treat Edge Pattern Line as the current active route and use this packet to line up the next cohesive chapter-building phase: review the route, deepen its transition content, add one front-half sheltered traversal proof, and then tighten the station board for three-route readability.
